data.body:
# Config-pruning assessment + epic 8d27 — investigation & work trail

## Objective
Assess rebar's config surface, answer specific backward-compat / feature-status
questions, and build an epic to prune settled config values, activate a completed
feature, and stand up authenticated-identity signing. Flag flips scoped carefully.

## Config inventory (source of truth)
- Project config: `rebar.toml`; full schema + defaults: `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`.
- No `.rebar/config.conf` override; only JIRA_* env set (mirror config, no feature flags).
- ON explicitly in rebar.toml: require_completion_verification_for_close,
  require_plan_review_for_claim, overlap_enabled, require_ticket_for_commit,
  mcp.allow_llm, reconciler.baseline_dual_write, reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap.
- ON by default (inherited): progressive_drift_refresh, remediation_mode,
  novelty_drop_active, sync.push/pull/remote, ensure.hint_enabled.
- OFF: require_signature_for_close, enable_code_review, completion_floor_active,
  identity.require_authenticated, emit_legacy_signature_mirror, mcp.readonly,
  mcp.allow_jira_sync, reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe.

## Investigation findings (five + three subagent dives, all cited to code)

### 1. Reconciler baseline configs (baseline_dual_write, baseline_consumer_swap) → always-on
VERDICT: backward-compat SAFE. Baseline is persisted ADDITIVELY into
`.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json` (no store-version bump; ADR-0026 says an
absent baseline is valid; old binaries ignore unknown keys). Reconciler is a single
advisory-locked GHA host process (no old-clone race). Neither flag changes the shape of
anything written to Jira or the tickets branch.
CAVEAT (safety, not crash): hardcoding consumer_swap on removes the shadow-warmup +
one-line rollback; a repo upgrading from a never-dual-wrote version hits
get_baseline()==None → "local-wins" arbitration until baselines populate lazily (could
silently revert a concurrent Jira edit). `seed_baselines_from_snapshot()` exists but is
never called. Moot for THIS project (dual-write already on).
Read sites: _config_schema.py:399,403,533,534; reconcile.py:938-954;
run_differs.py:458/473; outbound_differ.py:374/626/731/805; outbound_fields.py:380,418-419.
baseline_shadow.py becomes dead if going straight to always-consume.

### 2. compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror removal → CONDITIONAL (one-way door)
The key ALREADY defaults to never-emit, so hardcoding off changes nothing at runtime;
removal deletes the documented ROLLBACK lever (=true + recompact) that re-emits the
legacy `state['signature']` mirror for a still-live pre-attestations reader. Compaction
folds pre-horizon events into a snapshot and retires source events, so for pre-horizon
state the snapshot is the ONLY source; an old (pre-attestations) binary reading a
mirror-less snapshot sees no `signature`, can't read `attestations`, and can't re-derive.
Safe for THIS fleet (hourly auto-update ⇒ no old binary live). WOULD break an external
older-version project. Recovery after removal = code downgrade, not config.
Sites: _config_schema.py:367; compact.py:127-136 (_snapshot_strip_keys);
reducer/_processors.py:562-604 (process_signature re-derives in-memory mirror — PRESERVE);
signing.py:563-568 (most_recent_attestation fallback — PRESERVE);
docs/migrations.md:149-171 (readiness gate).

### 3. require_signature_for_close retirement — dead-code map
DELETE (reachable only via this gate): resolve_signature_gate (gates.py:58-80);
_signature_gate (txn.py:311-410) + its two CommandErrors; the transition_core plumbing
(txn.py:182-187, 250-260); config field+validator (_config_schema.py:210, :485);
true-path tests (test_signature.py:312, test_close_gate_story_epic.py:38,116,
test_idea_close_bypass.py:27 rewrite; config-test assertions; regen golden CLI help).
PRESERVE (shared): all of signing.py, the attestations map, sign_manifest/verify_signature
MCP tools, the completion-verifier close path (this project's actual close gate), and the
plan-review claim gate. --force-close / --verdict-hash NOT owned by this gate — keep.
Docs: manifest-signing.md:60, config.md:96,386, llm-framework.md:471, migrations.md:142,
release-notes.md:46, _cli/help/transition.txt:7, cli-reference.md:377.

### 4. Three verify defaults always-on (progressive_drift_refresh, remediation_mode, novelty_drop_active)
For each: delete field+validator + the `if not <flag>: return/None` back-out branch;
hardcode enabled path. KEEP tuning sub-configs (remediation_window_minutes,
novelty_drop_threshold, novelty_priority_floor) — plain params, independent, not orphaned.
KEEP config-unreadable try/except fail-safes + self-gates (no-prior-memory / `if sign`).
Sites: progressive: __init__.py:68-75, 490-497; remediation: __init__.py:79-95 (guard :92),
526; novelty (SHARED plan+code): __init__.py:206 + code_review/workflow_ops.py:609,
dispatched gate_dispatch.py:710-719. False-path tests in test_plan_review_gate.py,
test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py, test_plan_review_convergence.py,
test_code_review_region_gate.py, test_plan_review_rising_floor.py.

### 5. completion_floor_active — IMPLEMENTED + ALREADY CALIBRATED
Not incomplete: no stubs; wired live at __init__.py:536 in review_plan. Path:
_maybe_apply_completion_floor (__init__.py:312-359) → _classify_completion (:282-309) →
pass2_completion (passes.py:594-671, real LLM sub-call) → completion_floor_drop
(passes.py:674-715, deterministic 5-condition predicate) → _apply_completion_floor_to_verdict
(__init__.py:231-279). Story 77cf is CLOSED (delivered gold-set/eval only; code in dep
story 6533). docs/calibration/completion_floor.md: 30/30 decisions correct (κ=1.0), zero
must-never-suppress anchors dropped — "the evidence the operator needs before flipping on".
Risk of dogfooding LOW/bounded: advisory-only, container-only, 5-condition conjunction,
fail-safe-to-keep everywhere; cannot change PASS/BLOCK. Audit surface EXISTS: sidecar
dropped[] tagged drop_reason="completion" (__init__.py:263,272-275; sidecar.py:342,358).
Gap: successful drops not logged per-line (only failures warn).

### 6. identity.require_authenticated rollout surface
Gate mechanics: signed iff current identity resolves (.rebar/current_identity OR git
user.email match) AND identity.signing_key/REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY set
(_seam.py:264-325). Exempt types: session_log, code_review, identity.
NO Rebar Bot identity ticket + NO SSH authorship key exist yet (only HMAC key + Gerrit
deploy key). Must bootstrap ed25519 + identity ticket (email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com).
- Local dev: per-developer identity + key.
- AWS review-bot: writes only exempt types (voter.py:241,278) → NOT blocked; attribute only.
- AWS auto-lander: comments on WORK tickets (loop.py:310, non-exempt) → MUST sign. SSM.
- GHA reconcile-bridge: CRITICAL — inbound writer (inbound_translate.py _write_event_file
  ~214-259) calls _store.event_append DIRECTLY, bypassing the seam, stamps only `author`
  → events classify unknown-author/unsigned. NEEDS A CODE FIX (route through seam or
  stamp+sign). Also inject GH secret + flip BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL to the bot.
- GHA canary: uses normal CLI → auto-signs once identity+key set; flip committer email.
- CI verify-identity.yaml hard-forces --require-authenticated regardless of config; may
  not mount the tickets store (would exit 2). ENFORCE_SINCE grandfathering is the CI repo
  variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`, NOT identity.enforce_since.
Secrets: AWS SSM `/rebar/prod/*` via fetch-secrets.sh (ADR-0008); GitHub Actions secrets.

### 7. Test breakage under require_authenticated
Via THIS project's rebar.toml flip: NO breakage — test stores are isolated tmp REBAR_ROOT;
config discovery (config.py:46-67,532-544) never reaches the real rebar.toml; XDG stubbed
(conftest.py:189-199). Via global env REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=1: breaks broadly
(empirically every non-exempt create/mutate fails "cannot be signed"). Remediation:
autouse fixture in tests/conftest.py forcing the env to 0 (mirrors _gate_source_local_by_default
idiom :200-232); safe vs dedicated identity tests (they set their own subprocess env).

## Decisions
- Removals APPROVED: reconciler (#1), emit_legacy_signature_mirror (#2), the 3 verify
  defaults, require_signature_for_close. (Initially mis-scoped the mirror as KEEP;
  user corrected — their #1/#2 were the two backward-compat questions.)
- completion_floor_active: ENABLE to dogfood (complete + calibrated).
- identity: SETUP infra only; require_authenticated flag flip OUT OF SCOPE for now.
- compact mirror removal gated behind a readiness-gate assertion (one-way door).

## Epic built: 8d27 (cummy-monkeyish-dassie) — all open, not claimed
- d6bd — retire reconciler baseline configs → always-on
- 4cdf — 3 verify defaults always-on
- 28f1 — retire require_signature_for_close + dead code
- 7ed9 — retire emit_legacy_signature_mirror (readiness-gated)
- c366 — enable completion_floor_active (dogfood) + monitoring
- 297d — stand up identity signing (setup only), tasks:
    bffe bootstrap Rebar Bot identity+key (blocks 245e/9e76/1787);
    472f local dev; 245e AWS review-bot+auto-lander (SSM);
    9e76 GHA reconciler CODE FIX; 1787 GHA canary;
    ad42 test-guard remediation; cc0b confirm CI mounts store.
- 5566 — doc-accuracy sweep (depends_on d6bd,4cdf,28f1,7ed9)

## Execution notes for later
- d6bd/4cdf/28f1/7ed9 all edit _config_schema.py → shared branch or careful ordering.
- Remove retired keys from rebar.toml in the same change (else cosmetic unknown-key warning).
- Not claimed/worked — awaiting plan-review + dispatch.
